  • Launch Tools
  • Templates

Templates

Pre-built schemas for common document types.

Status​

Templates are coming soon. For now, define your custom schema using the schema_json parameter in /v2/process.

Planned templates​

When available, templates will include:

  • Invoice Extraction
  • Contract Analysis
  • Expense Reports
  • Email Extraction
  • Receipt Parsing
  • Form Field Extraction

Using custom schemas​

In the meantime, send any JSON schema to the API:

curl -X POST https://jsonparser-production-f9af.up.railway.app/v2/process \
  -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_PARSEFLOW_API_KEY" \
  -F "text=Invoice data here" \
  -F "schema_json={\"fields\":[{\"name\":\"invoice_number\",\"required\":true},{\"name\":\"amount\",\"type\":\"number\"}]}"

Check Examples for more schema examples. }


[View Template](/templates/email_extraction.json)

## Using Templates

### Option 1: Copy the Schema

Use the template JSON schema directly in your API calls:

```bash
curl -X POST https://jsonparser-production-f9af.up.railway.app/v2/process \
  -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_PARSEFLOW_API_KEY" \
  -F "file=@invoice.pdf" \
  -F "preset=extraction-focused" \
  -F "schema_json=@templates/invoice_extraction.json"

Option 2: Customize for Your Needs​

Download a template and modify it:

{
  "fields": [
    {
      "name": "invoice_number",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Invoice ID or reference number"
    },
    {
      "name": "custom_field",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Your custom field"
    }
  ]
}

Option 3: Use as Reference​

Study the template structure to understand how to build custom schemas.

Interpreting Confidence Scores​

Confidence scores range from 0 (not confident) to 1 (very confident):

  • Very High (0.9 to 1.0): Safe to use directly in automation
  • Good (0.7 to 0.9): Verify before using, consider secondary validation
  • Medium (0.5 to 0.7): Manual review recommended
  • Low (Below 0.5): High chance of error, requires manual verification

Recommended Presets​

For Templates​

  • extraction-focused: Best for accuracy (use for templates)
  • rag-optimized: Best for search/retrieval
  • summarization-friendly: Best for summaries

Chunk Sizes​

  • Small (1000-2000): Fast processing, good for short documents
  • Medium (2000-5000): Balanced performance
  • Large (5000+): Better for complex tables and multi-page documents

Common Use Cases​

Invoice Processing Workflow​

  1. Invoice email received
  2. Parseflow extracts fields using invoice template
  3. Automatically create accounting record if confidence greater than 0.85
  4. Flag for manager approval if confidence between 0.5 and 0.85
  5. Return to inbox if confidence less than 0.5

Time per invoice: Approximately 2 seconds

Contract Management Workflow​

  1. New contract uploaded to document management system
  2. Parseflow analyzes using contract template
  3. Extract key dates for calendar scheduling
  4. Flag high-risk clauses for legal review
  5. Update contract database with extracted terms

Time per contract: Approximately 5 seconds

Expense Automation Workflow​

  1. Employee submits expense report
  2. Parseflow extracts items and categorizes
  3. Auto-approve if total less than threshold and confidence high
  4. Route to manager if requires approval
  5. Update accounting system

Time per report: Approximately 2 seconds

Email Triage Workflow​

  1. Email arrives with action items
  2. Parseflow extracts deadline and action items
  3. Create tasks in project management system
  4. Send reminder to assignee
  5. Flag high-priority emails for immediate attention

Time per email: Approximately 1 second

Accuracy Tips​

To maximize extraction accuracy:

  1. Use high-resolution source documents - Clear, sharp text extracts better
  2. Define specific schemas - More specific schemas achieve higher accuracy
  3. Choose appropriate preset - extraction-focused is best for field extraction
  4. Ensure consistent formatting - Documents with consistent layouts extract better
  5. Test and iterate - Run sample documents to verify accuracy
  6. Set realistic thresholds - Account for document quality variations

Template Index​

All templates available in /templates/index.json for programmatic access:

{
  "templates": [
    {"id": "invoice", "name": "Invoice Extraction", ...},
    {"id": "contract", "name": "Contract Analysis", ...},
    {"id": "expense", "name": "Expense Report", ...},
    {"id": "email", "name": "Email Extraction", ...}
  ]
}

View Full Index

Create Custom Templates​

Use any of the above as a starting point to create custom templates for your specific documents:

  1. Copy an existing template
  2. Modify field names and descriptions
  3. Add or remove fields as needed
  4. Save as a new template
  5. Share with your team

Templates are just JSON schemas - the format is open and extensible.

Questions?​

  • See FAQ for common questions
  • Check Examples for API usage
  • Review API Reference for schema validation rules

Ready to extract? Choose a template above and try your first extraction.
